target.title: fix(agents): preserve delivery context through nested ACP completions
target.body:
## Summary

- Recover delivery channel from requester session entry when nested ACP completion origins lose their external channel after `stripNonDeliverableChannelForCompletionOrigin` strips a non-deliverable (internal) channel and neither `directOrigin` nor `requesterSessionOrigin` provide a deliverable fallback.
- Add `[warn]` log when completion delivery context resolves to no deliverable channel, making silent message loss detectable in gateway logs.
- Enrich `message-tool-only` delivery failure error with channel and requester info for easier debugging.

## Context

Addresses the remaining sub-issues from #67502 that were explicitly out of scope in PR #67785 (which fixed the global nested lane congestion):

1. **Delivery context corruption** — the session's delivery channel silently degraded after nested ACP runs, causing responses to route to the wrong target or be silently dropped.
2. **No detectability** — when delivery failed due to missing channel context, nothing was logged.

## Real behavior proof

**Before fix** — gateway logs from production Telegram session showing repeated delivery failures with no channel info:

```
2026-05-27T01:09:19 [warn] Subagent completion direct announce failed for run 2631a265: completion agent did not use the message tool for message-tool-only delivery
2026-05-27T01:09:22 [warn] Subagent completion direct announce failed for run 2631a265: completion agent did not use the message tool for message-tool-only delivery
2026-05-27T01:09:22 [warn] Subagent announce give up (retry-limit) run=2631a265 child=agent:main:subagent:c3f717e9 requester=agent:main:telegram:direct:REDACTED retries=3 endedAgo=30s deliveryError="completion agent did not use the message tool for message-tool-only delivery"
```

The agent generated a response (`stopReason: "stop"`, visible in session transcript) but it never reached Telegram. This repeated across multiple runs throughout the night:

```
2026-05-27T03:25:22 [warn] Subagent announce give up (retry-limit) run=b4c4e6ad ... deliveryError="announce deferred or direct delivery failed"
2026-05-27T04:55:49 [warn] Subagent announce give up (retry-limit) run=e26ef1d1 ... deliveryError="announce deferred or direct delivery failed"  
2026-05-27T05:57:48 [warn] Subagent announce give up (retry-limit) run=9d376781 ... deliveryError="announce deferred or direct delivery failed"
2026-05-27T08:28:26 [warn] Subagent announce give up (retry-limit) run=ac1ffaa0 ... deliveryError="completion agent did not use the message tool for message-tool-only delivery"
```

**After fix** — the enriched error now includes channel and requester info for debugging:

```
completion agent did not use the message tool for message-tool-only delivery; channel=telegram requester=agent:main:telegram:direct:REDACTED
```

And the new warning log fires when no deliverable channel is found, before the delivery attempt even starts:

```
[warn] Subagent completion delivery context has no deliverable channel; response may be lost. requester=... completionChannel=none directChannel=none sessionChannel=none entryChannel=telegram
```

**Environment:** macOS Darwin 25.5.0, Node 24.16.0, OpenClaw gateway (local mode) with Telegram channel, Claude Sonnet 4.6 via claude-cli.

## Verification

```
pnpm test src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts
# 71/71 passed

pnpm build
# ✓ built successfully

pnpm check:changed
# passed

pnpm changed:lanes --json
# core: true, coreTests: true
```

Behavior addressed: nested ACP completion delivery context preservation
Real environment tested: macOS, Node 24, OpenClaw gateway with Telegram channel
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm test src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts` (71/71 pass)
Evidence after fix: delivery channel recovered from session entry when effective origin has no channel; enriched error messages include channel info
Observed result after fix: all 71 tests pass including 3 new regression tests
What was not tested: live end-to-end Telegram delivery with a real nested ACP run that triggers the internal-channel stripping path (requires specific subagent spawn configuration)

Closes remaining sub-issues from #67502.
target.author: samuelsoaress

commands.author: rifachnia

target.title: fix: avoid gateway daemon repair on protocol mismatch
target.body:
## Summary

* Detect protocol mismatch explicitly during doctor gateway health checks
* Avoid treating incompatible running gateways as dead/stale gateways
* Skip daemon repair flow for protocol mismatch states
* Add regression coverage for protocol mismatch handling and repair skipping

Related #87156

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed:

* Protocol mismatch previously propagated as `healthOk=false`, causing `openclaw doctor` to treat a running gateway as dead/stale and enter daemon repair flow.

Real environment tested:

* Native Windows 11
* Git/source checkout
* Existing gateway service on port 18789
* Startup-folder fallback runtime active

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
openclaw gateway status
npm run openclaw -- doctor --deep
```

Evidence after fix:

* `gateway status` still reported:

  * `Connectivity probe: failed`
  * `protocol mismatch`
* but `doctor --deep`:

  * completed successfully
  * no longer printed `Gateway not running`
  * did not trigger daemon repair/reinstall flow

Observed result:

* Protocol mismatch is now treated as an incompatibility state instead of a dead gateway state.

Not tested:

* Scheduled Task migration flows
* Automatic gateway restart after update
* Non-Windows environments

## Tests

```bash
npm test -- src/commands/doctor-gateway-health.test.ts src/commands/doctor-gateway-daemon-flow.test.ts

npm run typecheck

npm run lint
```

Regression coverage added:

* protocol mismatch health classification
* skipping daemon repair flow during protocol mismatch

Validation result:

* targeted protocol mismatch tests pass
* oxlint clean
* no new typecheck errors in touched files
* one unrelated existing Windows test failure remains (`/tmp/` hardcoded handoff env expectation)
